Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the user-facing gallery/task surfaces against the actual repo and found that the plan's migration list still undershoots the stated goal. `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:174-199`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemTaskShare.ts:20-27`, and `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/components/VariantDetails.tsx:22-28` all read task state through `useGetTask`, so the gallery and variant UI still have direct task-query consumers outside the three named hooks the plan focuses on.
flags.evidence: Reframed the plan around the actual root cause, settled cold-start hydration explicitly, expanded the in-scope migration to the direct `useGetTask` gallery/variant readers, defined a full-record project-scoped store contract, preserved the status-only realtime context, and explicitly deferred `useActiveTaskClips` to avoid unjustified scope growth.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the architectural tiebreaker against the proposed steps and the current code. The plan generally respects the boundary rule by moving writes into `src/shared/providers/RealtimeProvider.tsx`, but it leaves the cold-start policy as an open question even though current counts/details depend on preexisting task rows before any new realtime event arrives; that requirement should be settled in the plan rather than deferred to implementation judgment.
flags.evidence: Reframed the plan around the actual root cause, settled cold-start hydration explicitly, expanded the in-scope migration to the direct `useGetTask` gallery/variant readers, defined a full-record project-scoped store contract, preserved the status-only realtime context, and explicitly deferred `useActiveTaskClips` to avoid unjustified scope growth.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:158-199` against the new store-backed aggregate plan. The current counts logic still applies `applyRootTaskFilter(...)` and visible task-type filtering before counting processing, recent successes, and recent failures; the revised selector plan talks about project scoping and time windows, but it never explicitly preserves that root-task exclusion, so a naive aggregate over all stored tasks would overcount child/orchestrator rows and change existing UI semantics.
flags.evidence: Refined the plan around the actual root cause, explicitly preserved `useTaskStatusCounts` derivation semantics and `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior, and restored stable selector references to a MUST acceptance gate without expanding milestone scope.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTasks.ts:58-61` together with `src/shared/lib/tasks/resolveTaskProjectScope.ts:1-13`. `useGetTask` is keyed off the resolved project scope, not just the raw `projectId` parameter, so a store-backed adapter that keys only by the caller-supplied project id would change behavior for null/empty-project callers unless the plan explicitly preserves that resolution step.
flags.evidence: Refined the plan around the actual root cause, explicitly preserved `useTaskStatusCounts` derivation semantics and `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior, and restored stable selector references to a MUST acceptance gate without expanding milestone scope.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched the broader single-task surface and found that the repo still handles this concept outside the named `useGetTask` readers. `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/taskDetails/useVariantSourceTask.ts:30-58` issues its own `taskQueryKeys.single(...)` query, and both `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskPrefetch.ts:23-107` and `src/domains/generation/hooks/tasks/generationTaskCache.ts:31-104` read or seed that same cache directly. The revised plan inventories the visible hook consumers more carefully, but it still treats the migration as if `useGetTask` were the only live single-task entry point.
flags.evidence: Retargeted the plan to cover every live single-task cache entry point, and split `useTaskStatusCounts` into current-project store narrowing plus query-authoritative all-project mode so freshness stays correct without widening realtime subscriptions.

flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the by-id prefetch path as well, because it is easy to miss when only thinking about generation-driven task lookups. `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/hooks/useVariantActions.ts` calls `usePrefetchTaskById()` on hover, and that helper writes `taskQueryKeys.single(...)` directly, so the executor needs to treat that path as part of the `useTaskPrefetch` migration even though the plan names the module more generally than the specific helper.
flags.evidence: Checked the by-id prefetch path as well, because it is easy to miss when only thinking about generation-driven task lookups. `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/hooks/useVariantActions.ts` calls `usePrefetchTaskById()` on hover, and that helper writes `taskQueryKeys.single(...)` directly, so the executor needs to treat that path as part of the `useTaskPrefetch` migration even though the plan names the module more generally than the specific helper.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the actual `useGetTask` callers and they commonly pass sentinel empty strings until a mapping resolves: `useGenerationTaskDetails`, `MediaGalleryItem`, `useMediaGalleryItemTaskShare`, `useGenerationDetails`, and `VariantDetails` all call `useGetTask(taskId || '', projectId)`. Any store-backed replacement needs to preserve the current 'disabled until both ids exist, then return null or task' contract, or these callers will subscribe or fetch against incomplete ids during the unresolved phase.
flags.evidence: Reframed the plan around the actual root cause, settled cold-start hydration explicitly, expanded the in-scope migration to the direct `useGetTask` gallery/variant readers, defined a full-record project-scoped store contract, preserved the status-only realtime context, and explicitly deferred `useActiveTaskClips` to avoid unjustified scope growth.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `useTaskStatusCounts` callers in `src/features/tasks/components/TasksPane/hooks/useTasksPaneController.ts:83-95` and `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/videoLightbox/useVideoLightboxEnvironment.ts:139`. The hook is used in both single-project mode and all-project mode via `{ allProjectIds }`, so aggregate selectors in the new store cannot just read a flat task map without explicit project scoping and reset semantics, or counts can leak across project switches and the Tasks Pane's all-project view.
flags.evidence: Reframed the plan around the actual root cause, settled cold-start hydration explicitly, expanded the in-scope migration to the direct `useGetTask` gallery/variant readers, defined a full-record project-scoped store contract, preserved the status-only realtime context, and explicitly deferred `useActiveTaskClips` to avoid unjustified scope growth.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Task status counts: `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts` still collapses every all-project query to `taskQueryKeys.statusCounts('__all-projects__')`, so different `allProjectIds` sets alias to the same cache entry even though the hook signature accepts arbitrary project lists. The revised plan says `useTaskStatusCounts({ allProjectIds })` remains correct and query-authoritative, but it does not mention this existing cache-key constraint, so that success criterion is still slightly under-specified if the milestone touches count wiring or tests.

flags.evidence: Added an explicit cold-start bootstrap path for pending-generation tasks, expanded the migration checklist and tests to cover `GenerationTaskContext`, and replaced the vacuous `RealtimeContext` gate with acceptance criteria that prove selector narrowing and bootstrap correctness.

flags.concern: Realtime context criteria: the MUST gate about eliminating task-state `RealtimeContext` consumers is already green in the current repo, so it does not provide milestone evidence.

flags.concern: Generation-task facade: `GenerationTaskProvider` remains an integration layer over `generationTaskCache`, so changing single-task hydration semantics without explicitly verifying that facade and its tests leaves supporting infrastructure coverage incomplete.
flags.evidence: Added an explicit cold-start bootstrap path for pending-generation tasks, expanded the migration checklist and tests to cover `GenerationTaskContext`, and replaced the vacuous `RealtimeContext` gate with acceptance criteria that prove selector narrowing and bootstrap correctness.

flags.concern: Task status counts: current-project store-backed counts still lack a concrete hydration source for recent successes and failures, so existing callers could regress to partial counts unless this path stays query-authoritative or adds a full-project bootstrap.

flags.concern: Realtime task writes: the revised provider step still underspecifies how new task inserts get enough data into the store, because processed task events strip away the full task row needed by generation-matching selectors.
flags.evidence: Narrowed the plan to keep `useTaskStatusCounts` query-authoritative, added an explicit raw-event and fetch-fallback hydration path for provider writes, and clarified that the new store only serves single-task and pending-generation selector narrowing.

flags.concern: Realtime task typing: raw task hydration can drift from fetched task snapshots because `src/shared/realtime/types.ts` still models a narrower `TaskRecord` than the canonical `TaskDbRow` mapper in `src/shared/lib/taskRowMapper.ts`.
flags.evidence: Explicitly bound raw realtime task hydration to the canonical validator/mapper in `src/shared/lib/taskRowMapper.ts`, clarified that store snapshots use canonical `Task` objects rather than `TaskRecord`, and added tests plus boundary rules to prevent partial realtime snapshots or ad hoc mappers.

flags.concern: Task status counts: `useTaskStatusCounts({ allProjectIds })` still aliases different project sets to the same `__all-projects__` query key, so the revised correctness criterion for all-project counts remains under-specified if this milestone changes count wiring or tests.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts` computes `cacheProjectId = isAllProjects ? "__all-projects__" : ...` and then uses `taskQueryKeys.statusCounts(cacheProjectId)`, which ignores the actual `allProjectIds` array even though the hook accepts it.
